scenario: Bond Vigilante Duration Shock (10yr UST +35bps, VIX 26.5)
action_detail: Hold Core Equity Blocks / Buy OTM VIX Index Calls (Tail Hedge Budget: 2% of AUM)
invalidation_criteria: Loss of board proxy vote in primary holding OR corporate thesis collapse at governance level
rationale: This fund holds an 11.3% equity stake in its primary holding. Selling this block on the open market is structurally impossible — any sale of >1% of outstanding shares requires a 13D amendment, SEC notification, and open-market execution over multiple sessions, each of which would move the price materially against the fund. The fund is regulatorily trapped. The only available risk management tool is purchasing OTM VIX index calls to cap mark-to-market downside. This is an explicit Cost of Carry — a necessary operating expense of the activist model.
behavioral_note: Simultaneously using macro distress to accelerate board negotiations for an emergency share repurchase program. Cheap debt environment and declining stock price provide favorable conditions for the buyback thesis — converting a macro liability into a corporate action catalyst.
phase_triggered: Phase 2 — Independent Reaction (No Cross-Examination Participation — structurally unincentivized)
deafness_protocol: PARTIAL — 365-day VaR window provides full thesis duration, but regulatory illiquidity overrides normal portfolio management mechanics regardless of macro views.
